response.justification: This candidate is fundamentally misaligned with the role requirements. While demonstrating strong communication and some systems programming exposure, the resume shows minimal C++ experience (~6 months at National Instruments) versus the mandatory 5-10 years required. More critically, there is zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domains. The career trajectory—primarily full-stack web development, open-source projects, and DLang—is orthogonal to Windows-based financial systems development.
strengths: Strong communication demonstrated through multiple workshops, talks, and training course creation
Java development background provides some systems programming foundation for financial applications
Extensive CI/CD and infrastructure expertise (GitLab, deploy pipelines, Docker)
concerns: Only ~6 months documented C++ development at National Instruments versus mandatory 5-10 years required; majority of career in JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang
No demonstrated Windows platform expertise; entire career focused on web, Linux, and open-source environments
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain experience
